2: MCP enables LLMs and agents to interact with real-world systems, access
3: dynamic information, and perform actions beyond text generation.
5: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is an open standard that facilitates communication
6: between Large Language Models (LLMs) and external systems. It employs a
7: client-server architecture, enabling LLMs to access resources, utilize prompts, and
8: execute actions through standardized tools. MCP allows LLMs to interact with
9: databases, manage generative media workflows, control IoT devices, and automate
10: financial services. Practical examples demonstrate setting up agents to communicate
11: with MCP servers, including filesystem servers and servers built with FastMCP,
12: illustrating its integration with the Agent Development Kit (ADK). MCP is a key
13: component for developing interactive AI agents that extend beyond basic language
